summary: Weekend scan of the latest available arXiv cycle, with no newer Sunday arXiv mailing found. The strongest relevant themes are Xe+Xe nuclear-deformation response, resonance regeneration and rescattering from small to large systems, AutoBNN fusion-barrier inference with uncertainty calibration, agentic lattice-QCD workflow generation, and decision-relevant uncertainty evaluation for physics ML.
strongest_paper: 2607.14422: AutoBNN extraction of heavy-ion fusion barrier distributions from sparse excitation functions, with Gaussian-process benchmarking, uncertainty calibration, measured-reaction demonstrations, and a released workflow.

papers.title: Responses of multiparticle observables to multidimensional nuclear deformation in relativistic heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: The paper studies how quadrupole deformation, triaxiality, and hexadecapole deformation in Xe+Xe collisions affect multiparticle flow and mean-pT correlation observables, then tests which deformation sensitivities survive iEBE-VISHNU evolution.

papers.title: Investigation of hadronic effects on resonance productions in small collision systems using the EPOS4 model
papers.authors: Hyunji Lim, Bong-Hwi Lim, Minjung Kim, Sanghoon Lim
papers.abstract: The study compares EPOS4 calculations with and without UrQMD to isolate regeneration and rescattering effects on resonance production from pp and p-O to O-O and Pb-Pb collisions.

papers.title: Bayesian Inference for Extracting Barrier Distributions from Fusion Excitation Functions
papers.authors: Aaron Philip, Pablo Giuliani, Kyle Godbey
papers.abstract: The authors use AutoBNN to infer fusion barrier distributions from sparse excitation functions with uncertainty estimates, benchmark against Gaussian-process regression, and apply the workflow to four measured heavy-ion fusion reactions.

papers.title: LQCDMaster: Agentic Scientific Computing for Lattice Quantum Chromodynamics Research
papers.authors: Haofei Gao, Tingjia Miao, Wenkai Jin, Muhua Zhang, Hanzhang Wang et al.
papers.abstract: A domain-specialized agent converts natural-language LQCD tasks into PyQUDA workflows using expert skills and deterministic Wick contraction, reporting exact reproduction of expert implementations in 63 of 70 benchmark tasks.

papers.title: Evaluating Epistemic Uncertainty: Beyond OOD Detection and Active Learning
papers.authors: Jakub Paplham, Willem Waegeman, Eyke Huellermeier, Vojtech Franc
papers.abstract: The paper evaluates epistemic uncertainty through decision-relevant risk, regret, and coverage surfaces and shows that proxy rankings can disagree with operational utility.

papers.title: LIGO-PINN: Learned Initialization via Gated Optimization to Alleviate Convergence Failures in Physics Informed Neural Networks
papers.authors: Nilay Anurag, Shital Adhikari, Taniya Kapoor, Nikhil Muralidhar
papers.abstract: The work targets PINN convergence failures by learning gated layerwise initialization and reports large improvements across 1D, 2D, fluid-dynamics, and 3D unstructured PDE tests, with code announced.

papers.title: RTS Smoother-Guided Learning of Physics-Based Neural Differential Models
papers.authors: Ahmet Demirkaya, Georgios Stratis, Tales Imbiriba, Zachary D. Danziger, Deniz Erdogmus
papers.abstract: The method alternates RTS smoothing of latent states with neural learning of missing ODE terms while retaining known mechanistic dynamics, with tests on partially observed linear, nonlinear, and stiff benchmark systems.

papers.title: Model-Independent and Data-Driven Extraction of the Photon Distribution Function at the Electron-Ion Collider
papers.abstract: The paper proposes a cross-section-ratio inversion method for extracting photon distribution functions at the EIC, but the abstract-level evidence does not yet justify strong model-independent or data-driven claims without realistic closure tests and uncertainty propagation.
